mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or pinned near zero, the discount rate on future cash flows stays compressed and equity duration rises. That supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month because valuation expansion dominates any slow growth drag.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in the majority of periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25%, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent baseline signals weakening household income and demand, which should pressure earnings expectations and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points versus its level 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if threshold-crossing episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average forward return is not lower than unconditional by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates stronger payroll income and retail spending, which lifts near-term earnings revisions and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal is not stronger than either single series alone, or if positive SPX returns do not occur in more than 55% of months where both series trend up simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with flat or falling CPIAUCSL indicates real output is improving without an inflation shock, which should be equity-supportive over the next quarter through higher margins and better cyclically sensitive earnings.
failure_condition: Reject if the spread between INDPRO growth and CPIAUCSL inflation does not predict positive 60-day returns, or if positive returns fail to exceed baseline when INDPRO rises and CPIAUCSL is stable or lower.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp upside surprise in CPIAUCSL forces markets to price a faster FEDFUNDS path, raising real discount rates and compressing equity multiples. That should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month when inflation is accelerating.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases by more than 0.4% month-over-month and FEDFUNDS is below 1%
failure_condition: Reject if CPI-driven tightening episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 40% of cases, or if the interaction term does not outperform either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST signals a housing-led demand slowdown that feeds into construction, durables, and consumer confidence, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as growth expectations are revised down quickly.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by at least 8% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if large housing contractions are not followed by negative 5-day returns more often than not, or if the average 5-day forward return is nonnegative in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth slows while UNRATE rises, the labor market is deteriorating in a way that reduces consumption resilience and earnings visibility. This combination should depress S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor deterioration signal does not produce lower 60-day returns than either series alone, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDPC1 growth after a contractionary phase indicates a cyclical rebound in final demand, which should support equity returns over the next quarter through improved revenues and risk appetite.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth turns positive after being negative in the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if post-contraction GDP rebounds are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the signal has no edge relative to unconditional returns over any 3-year rolling window.
